exercises.example_sentence: このボタンを押すと、機械が動きます。
exercises.translation: When you press this button, the machine operates.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら attaches to the た-form (押したら), not the dictionary form 押す shown here, so it can't fill this blank; it also frames a specific 'once you press it,' whereas と states the automatic result that always follows.
ば attaches to the conditional form (押せば), not the dictionary form 押す, so it doesn't fit here; it expresses a general hypothetical 'if,' while と marks the automatic, predictable result.
なら does attach to the dictionary form (押すなら), but it sets up a supposition about intention ('if you intend to press it'), whereas と describes the automatic effect of pressing.
exercises.hiragana_full: このぼたんをおすと、きかいがうごきます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: このボタンを押せば、機械が動きます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: このぼたんをおせば、きかいがうごきます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: このボタンを押したら、機械が動きます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: このぼたんをおしたら、きかいがうごきます。

exercises.example_sentence: 春になると、桜が咲きます。
exercises.translation: When spring comes, cherry blossoms bloom.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら attaches to the た-form (なったら), not the dictionary form なる shown here; it points to one specific arrival of spring, whereas と captures the recurring, natural cycle.
ても means 'even if' and would need the て-form (なっても); it sets up a contrast, but spring naturally bringing blossoms is an expected consequence, which と expresses.
から means 'because' and explains a reason, but the sentence simply links spring's arrival to the blossoms as a natural sequence, which is と's job.
exercises.hiragana_full: はるになると、さくらがさきます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 春になれば、桜が咲きます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: はるになれば、さくらがさきます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 春が来ると、桜が咲きます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: はるがくると、さくらがさきます。

exercises.example_sentence: 電気を消すと、部屋が暗くなります。
exercises.translation: When you turn off the lights, the room becomes dark.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら attaches to the た-form (消したら), not the dictionary form 消す shown here; it marks a one-time 'once you turn them off,' whereas と states the automatic result.
ば attaches to the conditional form (消せば), not the dictionary form 消す; it gives a general 'if,' while と marks the direct, automatic cause-and-effect.
ても means 'even if' and needs the て-form (消しても); it sets up a contrast, but the room going dark is the expected result of turning off the lights, which と expresses.
exercises.hiragana_full: でんきをけすと、へやがくらくなります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 電気を消したら、部屋が暗くなります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: でんきをけしたら、へやがくらくなります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 電気を消すと、部屋が暗くなる。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: でんきをけすと、へやがくらくなる。

exercises.example_sentence: 朝起きると、まず顔を洗います。
exercises.translation: When I wake up in the morning, I first wash my face.
wrong_choice_explanations: たら attaches to the た-form (起きたら), not the dictionary form 起きる shown here; both can mark a habit, but と presents it as the regular sequence that automatically follows waking.
ながら means 'while doing' and needs the masu-stem (起きながら); waking up and washing your face happen one after another, not at the same time, so と's sequence fits, not simultaneity.
でも means 'but/however' and marks a contrast (and needs the て-form or a noun); washing your face is the natural follow-up to waking, which と expresses.
exercises.hiragana_full: あさおきると、まずかおをあらいます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 朝起きたら、まず顔を洗います。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あさおきたら、まずかおをあらいます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 朝起きると、最初に顔を洗います。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あさおきると、さいしょにかおをあらいます。

exercises.example_sentence: 雨が降ると、道が濡れます。
exercises.translation: When it rains, the roads get wet.
wrong_choice_explanations: のに means 'although' and sets up a contradiction, but roads getting wet when it rains is exactly the expected consequence, which と expresses.
から means 'because' and explains a reason, but the sentence describes the natural result of rain rather than arguing why the roads are wet.
ので also means 'because' and gives a cause, whereas と simply links the rain to its automatic, natural result.
exercises.hiragana_full: あめがふると、みちがぬれます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 雨が降れば、道が濡れます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あめがふれば、みちがぬれます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 雨が降ったら、道が濡れます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: あめがふったら、みちがぬれます。

exercises.example_sentence: 12時になると、チャイムが鳴ります。
exercises.translation: When it becomes 12 o'clock, the chime rings.
wrong_choice_explanations: まで means 'until' and would describe the time leading up to noon, but the sentence is about what happens the moment it turns 12, which と marks as an automatic result.
ば attaches to the conditional form (なれば), not the dictionary form なる shown here; it gives a general 'if,' while と fits the regular, automatic timing of the chime.
なら does attach to the dictionary form (なるなら), but it sets up a supposition ('if it's a matter of it being 12'), whereas と states the automatic occurrence at that time.
exercises.hiragana_full: じゅうにじになると、チャイムがなります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 12時になったら、チャイムが鳴ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: じゅうにじになったら、チャイムがなります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 12時になれば、チャイムが鳴ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: じゅうにじになれば、チャイムがなります。

exercises.example_sentence: この薬を飲むと、眠くなります。
exercises.translation: When you take this medicine, you become sleepy.
wrong_choice_explanations: けれど means 'but' and marks a contrast, but becoming sleepy is the expected effect of the medicine, which と expresses, not a contradiction.
ても means 'even if' and needs the て-form (飲んでも); it sets up a concession, but sleepiness is the natural effect of the medicine, captured by と.
から means 'because' and explains a reason, whereas と simply links taking the medicine to its automatic effect.
exercises.hiragana_full: このくすりをのむと、ねむくなります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: この薬を飲んだら、眠くなります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: このくすりをのんだら、ねむくなります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: この薬を飲むと、眠くなる。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: このくすりをのむと、ねむくなる。

exercises.example_sentence: お湯を沸かすと、湯気が出ます。
exercises.translation: When you boil water, steam comes out.
wrong_choice_explanations: 間に means 'while/during' and points to something done in the course of boiling, but the sentence states the natural result of boiling, which と expresses.
ながら means 'while doing' and needs the masu-stem (沸かしながら); it marks simultaneous actions, but steam appearing is the result of boiling, not a parallel action.
ば attaches to the conditional form (沸かせば), not the dictionary form 沸かす shown here; it gives a general 'if,' while と marks the automatic physical result.
exercises.hiragana_full: おゆをわかすと、ゆげがでます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 水を沸騰させると、湯気が出ます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: みずをふっとうさせると、ゆげがでます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: お湯を沸かしたら、湯気が出ます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: おゆをわかしたら、ゆげがでます。

exercises.example_sentence: 右に曲がると、駅が見えます。
exercises.translation: When you turn right, you can see the station.
wrong_choice_explanations: のに means 'although' and sets up a contradiction, but seeing the station is the expected result of turning right, which と expresses.
ば attaches to the conditional form (曲がれば), not the dictionary form 曲がる shown here; it gives a general 'if,' while と naturally describes what you'll see when you turn.
なら does attach to the dictionary form (曲がるなら), but it sets up a supposition ('if you're going to turn right'), whereas と describes the automatic result of turning.
exercises.hiragana_full: みぎにまがると、えきがみえます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 右に曲がれば、駅が見えます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: みぎにまがれば、えきがみえます。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 右に曲がったら、駅が見えます。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: みぎにまがったら、えきがみえます。

exercises.example_sentence: 冬になると、雪が降ります。
wrong_choice_explanations: まで means 'until' and would describe the time up to winter, but the sentence is about what happens once winter arrives, which と marks as a natural occurrence.
から means 'because' and explains a reason, but the sentence describes the natural seasonal result rather than arguing why it snows.
ので also means 'because' and gives a cause, whereas と simply links winter's arrival to the snow as a natural consequence.
exercises.hiragana_full: ふゆになると、ゆきがふります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 冬になれば、雪が降ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ふゆになれば、ゆきがふります。

audio_alternatives.kanji: 冬になったら、雪が降ります。
audio_alternatives.hiragana_full: ふゆになったら、ゆきがふります。
